business.headline: Sensex ends 326 points higher, Nifty above 23,100; HDFC Bank falls 2%The S&P BSE Sensex added 325.72 points to end at 74,532.96, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 112.35 points to end at 23,114.50.

Zomato hikes food delivery platform fee to Rs 14.90 from Rs 12.50 per orderThe platform fee now stands at Rs 14.90 per order on a pre-GST basis, up from Rs 12.50 earlier. This marks another round of increase, with the last hike having taken place in September 2025.

Flipkart CFO Sriram Venkataraman to step down ahead of planned IPOVenkataraman joined Flipkart in 2015 and has been part of the company's leadership team through a period of growth and restructuring. During his tenure, he helped strengthen the company's financial operations.

Premium petrol prices hiked by Rs 2 per litre; regular fuel unchangedThe hike applies to high-octane fuels such as Indian Oil's XP95 and Hindustan Petroleum's Power petrol, based on dealer inputs from select cities.

Is the 20-minute neighbourhood the future of urban living?Urban planners across global cities increasingly speak of the "20-minute neighbourhood", a model where residents can access most aspects of daily life within roughly twenty minutes.

Buying insurance online? 8 in 10 users say it can lead to problemsA nationwide survey has found that more than 80% of users faced issues such as hidden charges, difficulty with cancelling policies, forced data sharing and misleading pricing while using online insurance platforms.

Gold prices today: Why are rates rising after a steep fall and should you buy?Gold prices are edging higher after a sharp fall, but the trend remains uncertain as global cues and a strong dollar continue to drive volatility.

HDFC Bank share price extends fall, down 2% after sharp sell-offThe stock slipped 2.19% to Rs 782.15 on the BSE at 10:35 am, adding to losses after it had plunged more than 5% in the previous session, marking one of its sharpest declines in recent months.

Rupee hits all-time low, slips past Rs 93 mark against US dollarThe rupee weakened to Rs 93.15 against the US dollar in early trade, extending recent losses amid rising geopolitical tensions and a surge in crude oil prices.

Sensex jumps 700 points: Why is stock market rising today? 3 key reasonsThe rebound comes as easing oil prices, positive global cues, strong buying in IT stocks, and broad-based buying after Thursday's sharp fall lifted investor sentiment.

Is Dubai's luxury housing boom starting to crack as Gulf conflict escalates?Dubai's property market faces a key test as tensions rise, sentiment weakens, and luxury villa deals come under pressure, with the outlook hinging on how demand and geopolitical risks evolve.
Sensex jumps 800 points, Nifty above 23,200; IT stocks rallyThe S&P BSE Sensex jumped 808.29 points to 75,015.53, while the NSE Nifty50 added 257.45 points to 23,259.60 as of 9:26 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market fall or rise today?Early indicators suggest a positive start. GIFT Nifty futures were trading at 23,250.5 at around 08:24 am, indicating that the Nifty 50 may open above Thursday's close of 23,002.15.
Ports power India's growth: Why sea trade matters more than everAcross the country, cities that have built strong linkages between ports and industry have often emerged as important economic hubs.
US issues fresh 30-day sanctions waiver for purchase of Russian oil at seaThe license, which expires on April 11, replaces and supersedes a similar 30-day sanctions waiver issued on March 12.

US says it may lift sanctions on Iranian oil stranded in tankers to calm pricesUS Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the addition of sanctioned Iranian oil into global supplies would help keep oil prices down for the next 10 to 14 days. Oil prices have been above $100 per barrel for much of the past two weeks as Iran has closed the Strait of Hormuz to shipping and has attacked tankers.

Iran may impose transit fees on ships to pass through Strait of HormuzSince the start of the US-Israeli war on Iran, Tehran has disrupted maritime transit through the strait for vessels it says are linked to its war adversaries and their allies.

Gold down nearly Rs 7,000, silver drops Rs 20,000: Buy, sell or hold now?Gold and silver prices plunged sharply today, with gold losing around Rs 7,000 and silver dropping Rs 20,000. This fall has buyers and investors debating whether to invest now or wait for a recovery.

Choice Mutual Fund rolls out two new fund offerings. Details hereThe two new schemes, i.e., Choice Nifty 50 Index Fund and Choice Nifty Next 50 Index Fund, are designed to give investors exposure to India's equity market without the complexity of actively managed funds.

Sensex crashes over 2,200 points as West Asia war escalatesIndian stock markets witnessed a severe crash as the Sensex plunged over 2200 points and Nifty fell by 687 points, down nearly 3 per cent, due to escalating conflict in West Asia.

entertainment.headline: Dhurandhar 2 in Pakistan: Viral video claims pirated film being watched in Lahore Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2 is allegedly drawing attention in Pakistan despite a ban on Indian films. A viral video has surfaced online that shows a man watching a pirated version of the film in Lahore.

Aahana Kumra cheers for Danish Pandor after Dhurandhar 2 success: Happiest for him Aahana Kumra celebrated the success of Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, praising the film's director and cast. Her special mention of Danish Pandor's impressive debut as Uzair Baloch caught significant attention amid rumours of their relationship.

Dhurandhar 2 seizes box office without a fight: Every record it broke so far Dhurandhar: The Revenge has turned its opening day into a full-blown box office event, delivering numbers that place it among the biggest in Indian cinema. With records already shattered and momentum building fast, the film's run is only just beginning.

Unconventional, bold use of craft: Director Shankar's praise for Dhurandhar 2 Director Shankar recently watched Dhurandhar: The Revenge and described it as an astounding experience blending real events with fiction. He praised the cast, music, and direction, calling it a historic moment for Indian cinema.

Shobhaa De reviews Dhurandhar 2: Arjun Rampal chewed up everyone, including Ranveer In her review of Dhurandhar 2, author and columnist Shobhaa De highlighted strong performances by Arjun Rampal and Sanjay Dutt. However, she found the film less thrilling than its prequel.

Dhurandhar 1 vs Dhurandhar 2: Which film owns the war it started? Is Dhurandhar: The Revenge better than its prequel? Aditya Dhar returns just months after the first film exploded on the screens, but does the sequel change the way we see this cinematic world? Here's a closer look.

Did Dhurandhar 2 actor Rakesh Bedi take a jibe at Kangana's Emergency? Read here Actor Rakesh Bedi reacted to Dhurandhar being labelled a propaganda film. He stressed that cinema should not be politicised. The actor compared its success with other political biopics.

BTS Swim music video X reactions: Fans can't keep calm over comeback track BTS' Swim music video triggered a flood of reactions online. Fans quickly turned the comeback track into a global talking point.

Prashant Tamang's death delays Salman Khan's Maatrubhumi release Salman Khan's film Maatrubhumi faces delay due to the untimely death of actor-singer Prashant Tamang, who played the main antagonist and had key scenes pending.

RGV calls Dhurandhar 2 a 'horror' for filmmakers, slams over-the-top action cinema Ram Gopal Varma called Dhurandhar 2 a horror for filmmakers relying on unrealistic action and exaggerated storytelling. He praised the film's grounded approach and Ranveer Singh's portrayal of a flawed hero.

Bharti Singh, Haarsh Limbachiyaa reveal second child Yashveer's face. See pics Comedian Bharti Singh and Haarsh Limbachiyaa have unveiled their son Yashveer's face after months of keeping it private. They shared photos on Instagram, which came hours after Yashveer's face reveal on their YouTube vlog.
BTS leader RM to limit stage performance at comeback live due to injury BTS leader RM suffers ankle injury ahead of ARIRANG comeback performance. The comeback live is scheduled for March 21.
BTS comeback album ARIRANG finds its emotional anchor in SWIM BTS drop ARIRANG, their first album in years. Led by 'SWIM', the record explores identity, growth and emotional resilience.

Mani Ratnam reunites with AR Rahman for Vijay Sethupathi and Sai Pallavi film Mani Ratnam has officially announced his next film featuring Vijay Sethupathi and Sai Pallavi, with music by AR Rahman.  The project is backed by Lyca Productions and is expected to be a romantic drama.

Sidhu Moose Wala's hologram tour Signed to God to cover 8 countries. See details Sidhu Moosewala's Signed to God World Tour 2026 will bring the late Punjabi icon back to the stage with cutting-edge hologram technology. It will cover 8 countries, including India, Pakistan, the United States, Canada and others.

Dhurandhar 2: Truth behind delay in release of film's South versions explained Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar: The Revenge saw its Hindi version release as per schedule, but the South dubbed versions faced delays. Despite the setback, the film crossed Rs 100 crore on opening day and is poised to break more records.

Chiraiya review: Divya Dutta's show is an unflinching take on marital rapeRelease Date:Fri Mar 20, 2026 Chiraiya series review: Chiraiya confronts the silent pain of marital rape and challenges deep-rooted patriarchy. Headlined by Divya Dutta, the series is now streaming on JioHotstar.

YouTuber Anurag Dobhal reveals injury scars, asks fans about comeback plans After a shocking crash and ICU stay, Anurag Dobhal shared an update on his recovery. It also raised questions about what comes next.

Dhurandhar 2 blatant propaganda: YouTuber Dhruv Rathee slams Aditya Dhar YouTuber Dhruv Rathee has strongly criticised Aditya Dhar's film Dhurandhar 2, calling it blatant propaganda and accusing the director of promoting a BJP agenda.

general news.headline: US troops to secure Hormuz, Kharg? New warship with 2,000 Marines on wayOver the past week, Trump has dropped enough hints of putting US boots on the ground in Iran. It could be just a matter of days. Satellite images have captured the USS Tripoli, an amphibious assault warship, speeding through the Indian Ocean. Is a ground offensive imminent?

Premium petrol prices hiked by Rs 2 per litre; regular fuel unchangedThe hike applies to high-octane fuels such as Indian Oil's XP95 and Hindustan Petroleum's Power petrol, based on dealer inputs from select cities.

World's first hit: How Iran likely took down 'unkillable' American fighter jetIran's air defences, which Donald Trump said were destroyed, hit an advanced stealth fighter jet like the F-35. It marks a significant event in the war as the US has boasted of decimating Iranian air defence capabilities. How did Iran manage to hit the US fighter jet?

TMC releases Bengal polls manifesto, mentions 'Didi's 10 pledge' to woo votersTrinamool Congress's manifesto promises welfare and development, adopting a voter-focused roadmap combining cash support with basic services like housing, healthcare and jobs.

Dhurandhar 1 vs Dhurandhar 2: Which film owns the war it started?Is Dhurandhar: The Revenge better than its prequel? Aditya Dhar returns just months after the first film exploded on the screens, but does the sequel change the way we see this cinematic world? Here's a closer look.

How a sailor's daily workout gave away French aircraft carrier's locationA French sailor accidentally compromised the location of the aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle by logging his workout on the fitness app Strava. The app's geolocation feature allowed real-time tracking of the carrier and its fleet.

Drone and doctrine: How Iran is able to counter-attack at time, place of choosingOn March 18, Israel struck a gas field in Iran. Tehran responded in a matter of hours, striking refineries in several Gulf countries. What explains this sharp, quick counter-attack capability of a country whose military infrastructure has supposedly been severely degraded? The answer lies in a cheap drone and a dispersed military.

Amazon is making a smartphone again, it will be big on AIAmazon is reportedly planning to make a re-entry into the smartphone market, going up against the likes of Apple and Samsung. The company's first attempt at smartphones with the Fire Phone led to a loss of $170 million in 2014.

15 days and done: The Gen Z attitude problem that millennials never hadFifteen days. That's all it took for a new hire to quit after a routine accountability conversation. When a founder summed it up as "skills can be trained, attitude cannot," it hit a raw nerve. Because, for many workplaces, this isn't rare any more, it's becoming a pattern, and one increasingly linked to Gen Z.

Live: Indian killed in strikes in Riyadh; Iran says war will go onUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Iran struck major energy sites in Saudi Arabia, Qatar and Kuwait, triggering fires and sending oil prices sharply higher, with Brent crude up more than 60% since the war began. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said Iran can no longer enrich uranium or build missiles, and confirmed Israel would pause further strikes on Iranian gas fields at US President Donald Trump's request. The Pentagon is seeking an additional $200 billion in war funding. Casualties continue to mount, with deaths reported in Iran, Israel, Lebanon and the West Bank, and over one million people displaced in Lebanon alone. More than 1,300 people in Iran have been killed during the war and at least 13 US military members have been killed. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for the latest updates.

One-second darshan at Deoghar's Baidyanath Dham triggers VIP-vs-commoner debateA clip of devotees being pushed in the sanctum of Deoghar's Baidyanath Dham made BJP MP Nishikant Dubey slam the Hemant Soren government. However, it has triggered a larger debate on the treatment meted out to common people and the privilege given to VIPs.

Iran executes 19-year-old wrestler Saleh Mohammadi with two others in public hangingIran hangs teen wrestling star, 19, in public execution with two others as rights groups warn of torture, sham trials and fear wave of mass killings to crush protests.
Massive cloud cover engulfs India as seen from space. See picsThe next 6-8 hours remain critical for northern states as the most intense segment of the trough lies in close proximity to the region.
With Pars on fire, humanity enters world of pain

Not surrender: Shashi Tharoor praises PM on Iran war, BJP amplifies remarkShashi Tharoor again deviated from the Congress line to praise PM Modi's diplomatic approach to the Middle East turmoil. The MP asserted that the government's 'restraint' reflected strength, not weakness.

Zomato hikes food delivery platform fee to Rs 14.90 from Rs 12.50 per orderThe platform fee now stands at Rs 14.90 per order on a pre-GST basis, up from Rs 12.50 earlier. This marks another round of increase, with the last hike having taken place in September 2025.

Why much of the Middle East is desert, but not Iran and TurkeyFrom endless dunes in Saudi Arabia to snowy peaks in Turkey, the Middle East is far more diverse than many assume. Climate patterns, mountain ranges and geography shaped where deserts formed, and why countries like Iran and Turkey look very different from the Gulf.

Keh ke maarunga: Iran named, then targeted assets in Gulf, humiliated USAfter its South Pars gas field was set on fire by Israel, Iran named and targeted critical energy assets in the UAE, Qatar and Saudi Arabia. With the 'keh ke maarunga' attitude, the IRGC didn't just display its power, but also humiliated the US.
Israel, US and Iran: The tail that wagged the superpowerHow Benjamin Netanyahu handed Donald Trump a war and a bill he may never stop paying

After 6 years, Sharjeel Imam walks out of jail on 10-day interim bailA Delhi court granted 10-day interim bail to activist Sharjeel Imam, an accused in the 2020 Delhi riots case. He walked out of jail on Friday after nearly six years in jail.

I'm not Jeffrey Epstein: Florida man reacts after viral lookalike videoA man from Florida has clarified that he is not Jeffrey Epstein after a video went viral last week in which viewers claimed he resembled the late sex offender.

Iran military spokesperson Naini killed in strikes soon after warning Israel, USEarlier in the day, Naini dismissed Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's claim that Iran can no longer enrich uranium or manufacture ballistic missiles, following nearly three weeks of coordinated US-Israeli air strikes.

Terrifying moment fodder-laden truck sways, overturns on highway in Madhya PradeshThe truck, loaded with straw beyond its capacity, began swerving dangerously while moving towards the temple stretch. Eyewitness visuals show the vehicle tilting and rolling sideways on the road as bystanders shouted for it to stop.

No evidence right now: Canada police chief on India's interference in OttawaCanada's national police chief said that there is no longer any evidence pointing to any foreign interference in reference to the Indian government on Canadian soil.

sports.headline: Tom Latham, bowlers script New Zealand's record T20I win against South Africa NZ vs SA, 3rd T20I: Tom Latham scored 63 off 55 as New Zealand beat South Africa by eight wickets at the Eden Park in Auckland to take a 2-1 lead in the five-match series.

India has one of the best indoor stadiums in South Asia: Sumariwalla India is set to host its first ever World Indoor Athletics Championships in 2028. World Athletics vice president Adille Sumariwalla reacted to the announcement and said that India has one of the best indoor infrastructure in the world.

IPL 2026: How have CSK treated their big buys? A story of injury and misfortune IPL 2026: Chennai Super Kings have a costly habit of big buys flopping. Can their 28.4 crore investment in youth finally break the curse in IPL 2026?

Aston Martin stand firm with Adrian Newey, dismiss speculations on rumoured exit Aston Martin insist Adrian Newey remains team principal amid reports the acclaimed designer is set to be replaced by Audi's Jonathan Wheatley at the struggling Silverstone outfit.

KKR audition ex-MI and RCB pacers as Harshit Rana replacements Kolkata Knight Riders have called up a host of fast bowlers for late trials, in the hopes of replacing Harshit Rana in the IPL 2026 season. Rana was injured in February and is yet to recover.

No media: How RR plans to shield Sooryavanshi from spotlight ahead of IPL 2026 IPL 2026: Rajasthan Royals will look to shield Vaibhav Sooryavanshi from media spotlight to ensure that the youngster can freely play his natural game ahead of the new season.

'Uncle' Shashi Tharoor recalls meeting 14-year-old Samson, Dhoni prediction Shashi Tharoor said that he is more like an uncle to Sanju Samson and recalled meeting the wicketkeeper when he was 14 years old. Tharoor also said that he had dubbed Samson to be the next MS Dhoni right after he met him.

Best in the world? Premier League clubs handed a European reality check LaLiga sides outclassed Premier League clubs in the Champions League Round of 16, exposing the gap between hype and execution. Spanish teams once again showed why they remained Europe's most reliable performers.

Starc joins Cummins and Hazlewood, set for delayed IPL 2026 start Mitchell Starc has joined Pat Cummins and Josh Hazlewood in having a delayed start to the upcoming IPL 2026 season. The decision was made as Cricket Australia look to manage the workload of their big three pacers ahead of a packed international schedule.

India vs Ireland T20I series confirmed for June, Stirling steps down as captain Ireland are all set to welcome India to their shores in June this year for a short T20I series, Cricket Ireland announced on Thursday, March 19. India will take on the Irish side ahead of their white-ball tour of England.

Sanju Samson, MS Dhoni's intense chat at CSK camp sets internet ablazeCSK fans are buzzing as new signing and India's T20 World Cup 2026 hero Sanju Samson was seen engaging in an intense chat with MS Dhoni ahead of the new IPL 2026 season on Thursday, March 19.

KKR receive Pathirana injury boost as pacer declared fit to play IPL 2026KKR has received a timely boost ahead of the IPL 2026 season as Matheesha Pathirana has received clearance from the Sri Lankan cricket board to link up with the franchise. Pathirana suffered an injury during T20 World Cup 2026 and was a big doubt for the upcoming season.

Pant firing in nets, champions do not stay out for long: LSG coach LangerIPL: LSG head coach Justin Langer believes that Rishabh Pant will fire on all cylinders in the upcoming season of the Indian Premier League. Langer said that great players do not stay out of form for long.

Mondo Duplantis in India? Bhubaneswar set to host World Indoor Champs' 2028Indian athletics fans could get a chance to watch pole vault superstar Mondo Duplantis on home soil, with Bhubaneswar set to host the 2028 World Indoor Athletics Championships.

Watch: Virat Kohli effortlessly takes down spinners in RCB netsIPL: RCB talisman Virat Kohli has started training at the M Chinnaswamy Stadium. Ahead of the IPL opener, Kohli was seen in some solid touch in the RCB nets.

CSK scamper for replacements as star pacer ruled out due to hamstring injuryIPL: Chennai Super Kings are hunting for a replacement for their star fast bowler Nathan Ellis. CSK CEO has confirmed that Ellis, has been ruled out of the season due to a hamstring injury.

Iran-Israel war's odd effect on cricket: England are running out of ballsEngland's domestic cricket season faces an odd disruption due to the Iran-Israel conflict. It seems that the war has hit the supply of Dukes balls, leaving counties with limited stock ahead of their new season.

Like Rohit Sharma and Virat Kohli, Sanju Samson cannot be replaced: Riyan ParagIPL: Rajasthan Royals captain Riyan Parag has said that like Virat Kohli or Rohit Sharma, Sanju Samson, is irreplaceable as a player. Parag is set to take over as the full time captain of the side this year.

PBKS will rectify mistakes from the IPL 2025 final: Shashank SinghIPL 2026: Punjab Kings all-rounder Shashank Singh emphasised that the side will work on their drawbacks shown in the 2025 final, and go the full distance in the upcoming season. PBKS lost the IPL 2025 final to RCB and missed out on a maiden title.

Never seen PCB this weak: Mohsin Naqvi and Co. slammed by ShehzadAhmed Shehzad has slammed the Pakistan cricket board and Mohsin Naqvi for enabling star culture within the team and allowing the players to take control of the national side. Pakistan suffered a Super 8 exit from T20 World Cup and were beaten by Bangladesh in the ODI series recently.

Watch: CSK's new star Sanju Samson arrives in Chennai ahead of IPL 2026IPL 2026: Sanju Samson has arrived in Chennai, and the buzz is already building. The new CSK recruit enters IPL 2026 on the back of a dream T20 World Cup, with expectations sky-high.

Gautam Gambhir moves Delhi HC against deepfakes, AI face-swapping misuseGautam Gambhir has moved the Delhi High Court against AI deepfakes and impersonation. The India head coach has sought legal protection over the misuse of his identity online.

Karthik, Pallikal blessed with 3rd child, Kohli, sporting world reactFormer India cricketer Dinesh Karthik and squash star Dipika Pallikal announced the birth of their third child on Thursday, March 19. Virat Kohli, Sania Mirza and others led the way with wishes for the couple on social media.

Knight Riders get a new home, become first franchise with own stadium in CaliforniaIPL 2026: The Knight Riders' global expansion has taken a concrete step in the USA. LA Knight Riders will now operate from a fixed base in California, becoming the first IPL-owned franchise to have a dedicated home ground.

Ishan Kishan as SRH captain is a beautiful story: Ex-India batter back IPL 2026 callIPL 2026: Ishan Kishan's rise continues to gather pace after his T20 World Cup heroics. Aakash Chopra has backed Sunrisers Hyderabad's decision to name him stand-in captain, calling it a fitting reward for his comeback journey.

Buttler hopes to keep England dreams alive after T20 World Cup 2026 flop showJos Buttler is hoping that he can continue playing for England, despite his poor showing during the T20 World Cup 2026. Buttler admitted that the final decision will be left in the hands of the selectors.

African football body chief defends decision to strip Senegal's AFCON titleThe African Cup of Nations 2026 final controversy has taken a new turn after Senegal were stripped of their title. CAF president Patrice Motsepe has denied corruption claims and defended the organisation's decision.

Arjun Tendulkar leaves Rishabh Pant impressed with Sachin-style technique in LSG campIPL 2026: Arjun Tendulkar has already made heads turn at his new IPL camp. The young all-rounder left LSG captain Rishabh Pant impressed with his bat weight and a technique that clearly reflects his father Sachin Tendulkar's influence.

Galatasaray's Noa Lang suffers freak finger injury vs LiverpoolGalatasaray endured a night to forget in the Champions League as injuries piled up during their heavy loss to Liverpool. Noa Lang suffered a freak finger injury while Victor Osimhen picked up a painful arm issue.

Hey Ancelotti, what about me? Neymar's reaction to Brazil squad snub goes viralNeymar's reaction to being snubbed from Brazil's squad for the friendlies against France and Croatia has gone viral as he jokingly asked Carlo Ancelotti why he wasn't in the final list.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
